figures.caption: Figure 1: Relative energy evolution for the accuracy test in Section 5.1. Left: comparison of entropy-conservative (EC) and entropy-stable (ES) fluxes with CCFL = 0.1. Right: entropy-conservative flux with CCFL = 0.01.
Table 1: Accuracy test for Section 5.1. Orders of convergence at t = 0.09 and t = 0.1. The reference solution is computed with 320 elements. CCFL = 0.1. Error is computed using (5.4).

Figure 2: Mean, standard deviation, quantile region, and augmented relative energy evolution for the near-dry perturbation test in Section 5.3. ∆x = 1/100. T = 1. Third order DG with KDG = 2 and KPCE = 4.
Table 4: Accuracy test for the perturbation-to-lake-at-rest problem in Section 5.4. Order of convergence for KDG = 2, KPCE = 2, T = 0.8. Reference solution computed with 800 elements. CCFL = 0.1.

Figure 3: Mean, standard deviation, quantile region of the water surface and discharge for the perturbation-to-lake-at-rest problem in Section 5.4 at T = 0.8. Left: Nx = 200. Right: Nx = 400. Third-order DG with KDG = 2 and KPCE = 4.
Figure 4: Augmented relative energy evolution for the perturbation-to-lake-at-rest problem in Section 5.4. Left: Nx = 200. Right: Nx = 400. Third-order DG with KDG = 2 and KPCE = 4.

Figure 5: Mean, standard deviation, quantile region of the water surface and discharge plot of Section 5.5. Left: solution with Nx = 200 elements. Right: solution with Nx = 400 elements. Third order DG with KDG = 2 and KPCE =K1K2 = 16.
Figure 6: Augmented relative energy evolution for the two-dimensional stochastic-input test in Section 5.5. Left: Nx = 200. Right: Nx = 400. Third-order DG with KDG = 2 and KPCE =K1K2 = 16.
